title: artemisia vulgaris subsp. michauxiana
intro: Artemisia vulgaris subsp. michauxiana, also known as American wormwood, is a subspecies of the common wormwood plant, a perennial herb native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. American wormwood is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and address specific health concerns.
